message:
fix(core): synthesize empty-set aggregate defaults in VDBE dispatch path

The VDBE dispatch path for SELECT execution (the else branch at the end
of the Statement::Select match) did not synthesize empty-set defaults
for implicit aggregates when the WHERE clause filtered all input rows.
This caused total() to return NULL instead of 0.0 and count() to return
NULL instead of 0 for queries routed through the VDBE codegen.

Add the same empty_aggregate_default fallback that exists in the
interpreted path (execute_select_after_background_status_and_subquery_rewrite)
to the VDBE dispatch path, handling both:
  1. Empty result sets (VDBE returned zero rows)
  2. Single-row results with NULL where a never-null aggregate is expected
     (VDBE returned a row but AggFinal was bypassed/wrong)

Also adds helper functions:
  - expr_contains_never_null_agg: recursively checks if an expression
    tree contains total() or count() aggregates
  - eval_empty_set_agg_expr: evaluates an expression tree substituting
    aggregate calls with their empty-set defaults, with special handling
    for typeof() wrappers

Reduces test_conformance_insert_select_dml mismatches from 3 to 2
(remaining 2 are the DELETE self-referencing subquery bug).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
